title: Electrolysis
summary: 9701 A Level electrolysis — electrode reactions, Faraday's laws, and predicting products in molten and aqueous cells.
learningObjectives: State what happens at anode and cathode during electrolysis.
Predict products for molten and aqueous electrolytes.
Apply Faraday's laws to calculate mass or volume of products.
Relate electrolysis to extraction and electroplating.

simpleExplanation.title: Forced redox with DC
simpleExplanation.summary: Electrolysis uses external voltage to drive non-spontaneous redox. Cations are reduced at the cathode; anions oxidised at the anode.
steps: Cathode (−): reduction — metal ions or H⁺ discharged.
Anode (+): oxidation — halides or OH⁻/H₂O to O₂ or halogen.
n = Q/F = It/F — moles of electrons → moles of product.
Molten: simple ion discharge; aqueous: compete with H⁺/OH⁻.

sections.content: **Electrolysis** (24.1) leads to **electrode potentials** (24.2). Practise **half-equations** and **Faraday** calculations — often combined in one question.
